adjudication_cascade.prompt: Apply approved/applied adjudication outcomes to claims registry and reopen dependents when cascade triggers fire.

architecture_epoch_applicability.prompt: Review stale-under-spec-change evidence and decide whether to retag, demote weighting, or retire stale runs from active conflict scoring.

architecture_structure.prompt: Review structure-pressure signals and decide whether to draft architecture changes.

items.rationale: At least one deterministic step failed or emitted warnings.
convergence_intake_queue_step=skipped; ready=10; pending_review=0; adjudicated=10; invalid=0; gate_failures=0; placeholder_evidence=0.
No structure-pressure or external-precedence triggers present.

autonomy_triage.prompt: Use this table to keep automation high while preserving explicit human ownership of architecture commitments.

conflict_resolution.prompt: Resolve directional/source conflicts before status promotions.

acceptance_checks: At least 3 primary sources per claim pull (peer-reviewed journals, major conference papers, or canonical preprints).
At least 1 disconfirming or mixed-direction source is included.
Each record includes mapping + confidence_components fields.
Summary includes source wording, REE translation, and explicit mapping caveat.
No duplicate DOI+claim linkage unless rationale is materially different.
items.connectome_focus: Map control-plane cognitive modes to empirically constrained network-control regimes and transition signatures.
items.objective: Run targeted connectome literature pull for ARC-016.
items.plain_english_claim_description: ARC-016 is an architecture commitment about cognitive modes / control plane regimes.
items.plain_english_ree_fit_description: This is in REE's architecture layer and constrains mechanism choices. It depends on 3 upstream claim(s): `ARC-005`, `MECH-059`, `ARC-021`. It currently influences 14 downstream claim(s): `ARC-029`, `INV-033`, `MECH-025`, `MECH-026`, `MECH-027`, `MECH-028`, `MECH-039`, `MECH-041`, `MECH-097`, `MECH-104`, `MECH-106`, `MECH-114`, `MECH-120`, `MECH-123`. Primary anchor: `docs/architecture/modes_of_cognition.md#arc-016`.

research_questions: Which large-scale network control findings support regime-like mode organization over shared predictive machinery?
What effective-connectivity signatures best mark transitions between task, DMN-like, vigilance, and offline regimes?
Which findings contradict strict discrete-mode assumptions and imply quasi-continuous landscapes?

query_stems: triple network salience switching default mode executive control
effective connectivity mode transition salience network
connectome gradient default mode control network transition
network controllability cognitive state transitions connectome

search_tracks.focus: Disconfirming evidence for strict regime separability
query_stems: continuous cognitive state manifold versus discrete modes fMRI
dynamic functional connectivity gradual transitions evidence

required_record_contract.summary: include caveats, disconfirming evidence, and mapping limits

high_priority_proposals.design: 2x2: condition A = committed BG gate active, condition B = gate ablated (beta suppressed), crossed with stable vs volatile environment. 2 matched seeds. PASS: harm_committed < harm_ablated in stable env; gap narrows or reverses in volatile env.
high_priority_proposals.notes: Depends on ARC-016 (provisional) and MECH-090 (active). This is the behavioral consequence layer split from ARC-016 into ARC-029 during 2026-03-22 governance session.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Test whether committed operating mode produces measurably lower harm outcomes than ablated (uncommitted) mode, and whether this gap narrows in volatile environments (gate-ablation design).

key_sources: Balleine & O'Doherty (2010) - Human and rodent homologues in action control: corticostriatal determinants of goal-directed and habitual action
DLS/DMS lesion studies distinguishing outcome-sensitive vs stimulus-response behavior
Beta oscillation persistence during committed action sequences (Khanna & Carmena 2015 or similar)
Daw et al. (2005) - Uncertainty-based competition between prefrontal and dorsolateral striatal systems for behavioral control
high_priority_proposals.objective: Ground ARC-029 in habitual vs goal-directed neuroscience: dorsolateral vs dorsomedial striatum dissociation, beta persistence during habitual sequences, model-free/model-based dual-process accounts.

key_sources: Gallese et al. (1996) / Rizzolatti & Craighero (2004) - Mirror neuron system and action understanding
Kording & Wolpert (2004) - Bayesian integration in sensorimotor learning (causal inference framework)
Decety & Lamm (2007) - The role of the right temporoparietal junction in social interaction
Wolpert, Ghahramani & Jordan (1995) - An internal model for sensorimotor integration (efference copy)
high_priority_proposals.objective: Ground MECH-072 (foreseeable-harm gating reduces false attribution) in causal attribution neuroscience: mirror neuron agency detection, Bayesian causal inference, TPJ role in intentional attribution, cerebellar efference copy in self/other discrimination.

high_priority_proposals.design: Condition A = harm-surprise-gated beta variance (unexpected harm spike triggers variance increase). Condition B = jitter-only control (no harm-triggered spike). PASS: A shows significantly higher beta_variance on unexpected harm events than B; B shows stable low-level jitter only.
high_priority_proposals.notes: EXQ-049e PASS validated Route-1 (noise floor preventing variance collapse). This experiment validates Route-2 (harm-surprise triggering volatility spike). Both routes needed for full MECH-104 validation.
high_priority_proposals.objective: Validate Route-2 MECH-104 wiring: unexpected harm events should spike commitment uncertainty (beta variance) via surprise gate, distinct from the jitter noise floor baseline.

key_sources: Yu & Dayan (2005) - Uncertainty, neuromodulation and attention (expected vs unexpected uncertainty)
Aston-Jones & Cohen (2005) - An integrative theory of locus coeruleus-norepinephrine function: adaptive gain and optimal performance
Sara (2009) - The locus coeruleus and noradrenergic modulation of cognition
Bouret & Sara (2005) - Network reset: a simplified overarching theory of locus coeruleus noradrenaline function
high_priority_proposals.objective: Deepen biological grounding of MECH-104 (LC-NE volatility interrupt): Yu & Dayan 2005 expected/unexpected uncertainty distinction, Aston-Jones & Cohen adaptive gain theory, Sara norepinephrine and memory consolidation, Bouret & Sara network reset hypothesis.

governance_decisions.prompt: Review promotion/demotion queue and record decisions.

human_decision_briefs.prompt: Review claim-level human decision briefs with mechanism context and metric glossary before recording any human decision.

maintenance.prompt: Address ingestion hygiene warnings and unlinked evidence runs.

manual_carryover.prompt: Carry forward unfinished manually-tracked governance items.

model_adjudication.prompt: Resolve JEPA-vs-REE conflicts using explicit outcomes and apply cascade rules when REE claims are replaced.

structure_review_dossiers.prompt: Review claim dossiers before deciding architecture changes.
